title: Man arrested in theft case dies in ‘police custody’ in Nellore district
content: Family members and relatives allege that he died due to ‘third-degree torture’ for three days while in custody at Manubolu police station

title: One prison escapee arrested after police open fire in Kalaburagi
content: The arrested prisoner was lodged in the prison in connection with a case under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act

title: A cathedral revives debate on Kerala’s unique church architecture
content: The newly renovated St. Mary’s Orthodox Cathedral in Puthiyakavu, Mavelikara, is set to be consecrated on July 17 and 18. Established in AD 943, the cathedral blends Portuguese Baroque and traditional Kerala designs, showcasing a 2,000-year cultural evolution among Saint Thomas Christians.

title: Call to treat waste management as a global concern, not a local issue
content: Development activities should be planned and implemented to benefit both present and future generations, says Kerala Sastra Sahitya Parishad president

title: BJP for protecting quota for ‘genuine SCs’, says its State president Madhav
content: The term ‘Dalit Christian’ has no constitutional basis and should be discontinued, he says, adding that benefits meant for SC communities are being misused by those who converted to Christianity

title: Farmers stage protest in Belagavi seeking fair land compensation
content: Farmers from Mastaholi, surrounding villages staged the protest with cattle at the office of Karnataka Neeravari Nigam Limited North Zone

title: Cauvery Water Regulation Committee decides to wait till July 28 to review situation
content: Karnataka’s representatives have argued that the State was not in a position to release the required quantum of Cauvery water to Tamil Nadu on the grounds that the Cauvery basin was facing water shortage

title: EC to hold special weekend campaigns as SIR extended till August 8
content: Under the revised programme, house-to-house visits by BLOs will continue till August 8, the same day on which the rationalisation and reorganisation of polling stations will be completed

title: ECI gives Ritabrata faction of Trinamool another extension till July 25 to file response
content: Ritabrata’s group was given an extension till July 10, which was subsequently extended till July 25 following yet another representation from them seeking more time; Mamata’s faction has questioned the additional time given by the ECI to Ritabrata’s faction

title: Thrissur Corporation hands over ₹50-lakh copper wire theft case to Vigilance after six years
content: Decision after investigations by the police and the Crime Branch into the alleged theft of copper wires from the Thrissur Corporation Electricity department’s Paravattani store failed to make any headway in the case

title: Army entrance test results declared; recruitment rallies from August 1
content: Candidates seeking recruitment as Agniveer General Duty, Technical or Tradesmen (10th and 8th pass categories) will participate in rallies in Visakhapatnam from September 3, and Ongole from February 1, 2027.

title: Worker rolls shrink by 67.6 lakh in first fortnight after MGNREGS transition, finds LibTech
content: Analysis finds decline in registered and active workers coinciding with shift to VB-G RAM G; Rural Development Ministry counters claim saying it has received no complaints

title: A.P. CM Chandrababu Naidu reviews progress of Amaravati Integrated Master Plan
content: Senior officials from the Government of Singapore explain the scope of the project and the action plan, which includes area development, infrastructure planning and investment models

title: UNESCO Award-winning restoration of Thukkachi temple in Tamil Nadu: a blend of traditional wisdom and modern technology
content: On December 6, 2024, UNESCO announced its Award of Distinction, recognising the Thukkachi restoration as an outstanding example of conserving a living heritage monument while preserving its historical authenticity.

title: 313 candidates to get degrees at IIT Dharwad convocation on Saturday
content: Undergraduate, postgraduate and doctoral degrees and diplomas will be presented during the seventh annual convocation of the institute

title: Kerala HC reserves order on apology tendered by Cashew department Secretary
content: Court asks K. Biju to apologise after the order issued by him giving sanction to prosecute two former officials of the KSCDC allegedly made accusatory remarks against the judiciary

title: Files relating to Kudankulam nuclear power plant exposed in data breach: report
content: Anil Ambani’s Reliance Group, one of the plant’s contractors, told Reuters in a statement that there had been a “partial breach” of its data on a server hosted by third-party Indian data centre service provider Yotta, and that the govt. has been informed about the incident

title: ESIC nursing officers begin black badge protest
content: The nursing officers are protesting against what they describe as illegal, arbitrary and large-scale mandatory transfers implemented under the Annual General Transfer (AGT)-2026.

title: T.N. Medical Services Corporation invites bids for Thaimaman Thanga Mothiram scheme
content: According to the order, gold rings would be procured for 4,41,667 deliveries at a total cost of ₹755.83 crore — ₹17,000 each — along with ₹5 crore towards administration costs

title: Veligonda project will be dedicated to nation on September 1, says Andhra Pradesh CM Chandrababu Naidu
content: Calling for interlinking of rivers to avoid water going waste into the sea, the Chief Minister expresses his resolve to provide water to every acre in the State

title: KADA Project Director calls for swift action on key infrastructure projects
content: Inspecting the Officers’ Guest House under construction on Gudupalle road, Vikas Marmat directs officials to ensure that all recommendations are incorporated while maintaining high construction standards

title: BRS hits out at double standards of NDSA to victimise Telangana ryots
content: Former MP Vinod Kumar says Polavaram suffered damage 13 times costing ₹9,600 cr. for repairs, but NDSA is yet to submit its report

title: Bidadi is not my dream project, says Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar
content: Shivakumar recalled that at a meeting chaired by then Chief Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy on September 23, 2006, the government had approved the development of five integrated townships around Bengaluru

title: Mysuru: Yathindra Siddaramaiah hints at induction of new faces into Cabinet
content: Mr. Yathindra said the Cabinet expansion had been delayed owing to elections to the Legislative Council and the Rajya Sabha, but expressed hope that the exercise would be taken up soon.

title: A.P. secures textile investments worth ₹4,100-crore at ‘Bharat Tex 2026’
content: Agreements signed under the leadership of Commissioner of Andhra Pradesh Handlooms and Textiles, K. Rekha Rani, as part of the State’s efforts to attract investments into the textile sector

title: Fake IT emails target Kerala chitty firms; association seeks police crackdown on cyber fraud
content: All Kerala Chitty Foremen’s Association says fraudsters have been sending emails, pretending to be Income Tax officials, demanding that institutions submit key financial records and other confidential documents within three days, warning of legal action and penalties for non-compliance

title: Madras High Court to examine NGT’s construction ban on 1 km radius around Pallikaranai marshland on August 3
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan agree to take up CREDAI-Chennai’s case for final hearing

title: Kerala Bird Atlas 2.0 launched
content: The second edition of the citizen science initiative will be done twice a year, from mid July to mid September, and from mid January to mid March.

title: Yathindra Siddaramaiah reviews preparations for Ashada Fridays at Chamundi Hills in Mysuru
content: Though arrangements had been made for VIP darshan on Ashada Fridays, Mr. Yathindra said there would be no inconvenience to other devotees.

title: Aqua syndicate dictating terms in Andhra Pradesh, alleges Jagan
content: The YSRCP president accuses the NDA government of pushing the sector into distress with its anti-farmer policies

title: ED files chargesheet in alleged U.S. visa fraud racket
content: The ED’s probe is based on multiple FIRs alleging conspiracy in the preparation of forged educational certificates, fabricated experience certificates, fake financial statements, and fraudulent proof of funds for securing U.S. student and visitor visas

title: World Plastic Surgery Day celebrated at Guntur GGH
content: GGH Superintendent highlights role of plastic surgeons in trauma care, cancer reconstruction and limb-saving procedures

title: Bidadi township: Committee to cool off rising political tensions?
content: The proposed committee is expected to provide a cooling-off period and signal that the government is willing to address the concerns raised by farmers and other stakeholders

title: Kerala plans to start electric boat service through Parvathy Puthanar ahead of Onam
content: The initiative, spearheaded by the Transport department, aims to revitalise the 19th-century waterway as part of the larger Kovalam–Bekal National Waterway project, providing a scenic route for commuters and tourists alike.

title: Registration campaign launched for MY Bharat portal in Mysuru
content: Through the portal, people aged between 15 and 29 can access employment opportunities, skill development programmes and internships free of cost, says Mysuru Additional Deputy Commissioner

title: Hyderabad Metro Rail to explore additional coaches, improve connectivity
content: HMR additional MD has called for coordination with Telangana State Road Transport Corporation and Transport department to explore suitable solutions for improving first- and last-mile connectivity

title: House sites, land titles sought for poor in Mangalagiri constituency
content: Residents led by CPI(M) leaders stage a sit-in on the old Vijayawada-Mangalagiri highway near KL Rao Colony, disrupting traffic

title: Probe into railway signal failure in Ambattur-Avadi section exposes violation of safety rules
content: Documents accessed by The Hindu reveal that the automatic signal, S192, malfunctioned over 50 times in February 2026 alone. A buzzer installed in the Ambattur Station Master’s office to alert the staff to signal failures was found to have been “disconnected”. The defective signal was repeatedly reset by field technical workers without analysing the root cause of the malfunction

title: As Sonam Wangchuk hangs on to hunger strike, weight loss approaches 9 kg
content: On the 18th day, activist Sonam Wangchuk continued his hunger strike demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, even as his vital signs showed a worsening condition

title: Over 500 devotees take ‘Varahi Deeksha’ vow in Tirupati district
content: The nine-day festival gets off to a traditional start with rituals like ‘Deepa Sthapana’, ‘Ganapati Puja’, ‘Kalasa Puja’, Maha Sankalpam’ and ‘Varahi Mahayantra Puja, after which the devotees accepted the vow

title: No policy paralysis, government is proactive in policy making: Revanth Reddy
content: Chief Minister inaugurates Amara Raja Advanced Cell Technologies’ Customer Qualification Plant (CQP) in Divitipally, near Hyderabad

title: Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan urges effective implementation of POCSO Act
content: In his letters to Telangana Chief Minister Revanth Reddy and Andhra Pradesh Chief Secretary Sai Prasad, he cites the recent murders in Shabad mandal in the neighbouring State, which, he says warrant steps that ensure the implementation of the Act in letter and spirit

title: 'Abhishek has done no wrong': Mamata stands behind nephew amid rebellion in TMC
content: Mamata Banerjee strongly defended Abhishek Banerjee, stating he had done no wrong. She accused rebel leaders of unfairly targeting her nephew and his family. Mamata linked Madan Mitra's exit to ED summons, suggesting a 'setting' with the BJP. She asserted that a few leaders leaving would not weaken the Trinamool Congress party. The party chief vowed to rebuild the organization and continue its political fight.

title: 'India needs your voice': Shashi Tharoor urges Sonam Wangchuk to end hunger strike
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor has publicly backed students protesting against examination irregularities, urging climate activist Sonam Wangchuk to stop his hunger strike. He pointed out that equitable exams are vital for young people from less privileged backgrounds. Tharoor affirms that peaceful demonstrations are necessary to amplify youth concerns, and the opposition is set to raise these student issues in Parliament next week.

title: 'Bumrah cannot be the answer to all your problems': Gautam Gambhir warned
content: India began the ODI series with a six-wicket victory against England. Jasprit Bumrah and Prasidh Krishna led the bowling attack effectively. Joe Root and Liam Dawson provided a strong recovery for the hosts. Shubman Gill and Axar Patel guided India to a successful chase. The team took a 1-0 lead in the three-match series.

title: 'Next comes bridges, power plants': Trump says US strikes on Iran to escalate in 3 days
content: President Trump convened a meeting to discuss expanding military operations against Iran. The United States is currently conducting strikes targeting Iranian air defense systems. Trump warned of intensified attacks on power plants and bridges if Iran does not negotiate. Washington is also monitoring a deeply buried site believed to be part of Iran's nuclear program. Iran has continued retaliatory attacks on US military bases in the region.

title: Demis Hassabis warns of AI model that could ‘break the world,’ Satya Nadella replies
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis has called for a US-led AI watchdog with the power to test frontier models before release and coordinate an industry-wide slowdown if risks mount. Modelled on financial regulator FINRA, the industry-funded body drew quick backing from Microsoft's Satya Nadella, who warned against any "one model drop that breaks the world," plus Sam Altman, Mustafa Suleyman and Sundar Pichai.

title: Over 10 million sign petition to kick Argentina out of FIFA World Cup amid referee bias claims
content: More than 10 million people have signed a petition calling for Argentina's expulsion from the 2026 FIFA World Cup, alleging referee bias in favour of Lionel Messi's side after their comeback win over Egypt. Egypt accused FIFA of unfair officiating, while Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni rejected the claims, insisting VAR and modern technology make favouritism "next to impossible".

title: Agra home sees rush of devotees after refrigerator ice takes Shivling-like shape
content: A refrigerator ice formation in Agra has drawn many visitors after appearing like a Shivling. News of the unusual ice spread, leading to neighborhood residents gathering at the home. Devotees performed rituals and chanted religious slogans, seeing it as a divine sign. This event coincides with the Amarnath Yatra, prompting comparisons to the cave shrine's ice. The home has become a local attraction for both devotees and curious onlookers.

title: Delhi techie returns to India after 7 years in US, says 'don't regret my decision'
content: An Indian tech professional returned to India after seven years abroad. He found lived reality in India differed from online narratives. The engineer cited visa issues and family as reasons for his return. He noted challenges in the US, including homelessness and racism. His experience suggests adapting to local realities is key in any country.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: Delhi allows taller buildings near metro, railway stations: What changes
content: Delhi's newly introduced transit-oriented development policy promotes increased density near metro corridors. This initiative permits mixed-use construction within a 500-meter radius of transit routes. By paying designated charges, developers can enhance their floor area ratio. Projects must maintain a balanced land-use mix and comply with updated parking regulations. The Delhi Development Authority is responsible for overseeing the implementation and approval of these projects.

title: China’s straw grids are helping deserts shrink by over 1,000 sq km a year
content: China's Green Great Wall project has fought desertification for nearly fifty years.  Millions of rural laborers have used straw checkerboards and planted saplings.  This effort has reduced desertified land and increased forest cover significantly.  The program demonstrates a long-term political commitment to environmental restoration.  Sustaining these gains requires continued local involvement and adaptation.

title: Indian artist cycled from India to Sweden for love in the 1970s
content: An Indian art student met a Swedish traveler in Delhi, and their connection grew quickly. He promised to follow her to Sweden after finishing his studies. Selling all possessions, he cycled thousands of kilometers across continents to reach her. His journey involved overcoming many hardships and relying on his art for survival. The couple eventually married and built a life together in Sweden.

title: Oracle rating downgraded by S&P, now stands one notch above junk; reason is Sam Altman
content: S&P Global Ratings lowered Oracle's credit rating to BBB- from BBB.  The agency cited significant risks associated with Oracle's expanding AI infrastructure business.  OpenAI's substantial reliance on Oracle's services presents a key credit risk for the company.  Oracle faces potential financial strain from massive data center leases if OpenAI defaults.  The rating agency warned of further downgrades if leverage exceeds 4.5x.

title: No rating-based spots! FIDE launches new qualification system for 2028 Candidates
content: FIDE has approved a revamped qualification system for the 2028 Open and Women’s Candidates Tournaments, introducing the Total Chess World Championship Tour as a new pathway while removing rating-based qualification. World Cup spots have been reduced to two finalists, and FIDE Circuit places will be decided over a two-year cycle, rewarding consistent performances across major events.

title: ICC introduces 'Super Series' and 'Super 7' format for 2027 ODI World Cup
content: The International Cricket Council has approved significant format changes for its major men's tournaments. The ODI World Cup will now feature a three-stage format before the knockout rounds begin. The T20 World Cup will expand to twenty teams with a new Super 10 stage. A new qualification pathway for the 2028 T20 World Cup has also been approved by the council.

title: Man punches glass door at Ghaziabad police booth after waiting 40 mins, bleeds to death
content: A 22-year-old man died from bleeding outside a police booth after no response. He injured his hand punching a glass door in frustration while seeking help. Two women constables inside the booth did not open the door due to his aggressive behavior. A police patrol arrived and took the injured man to the hospital where he was declared dead.

title: Historic! Rodri breaks 60-yo record, becomes first-ever player in FIFA WC to ...
content: Spain's Rodri made FIFA World Cup history by completing 655 passes at the 2026 tournament — the most by any player in a single edition since 1966. The Ballon d'Or winner starred in Spain's 2-0 semi-final victory over France, producing a dominant midfield display as La Roja reached their first World Cup final since 2010.

title: Meet the 95-year-old Nonno whose simple band workout keeps him strong and active
content: A 95-year-old grandfather, Nonno, demonstrates his daily exercise routine for staying fit. He uses a resistance band for low-impact strength training, showing great control. His granddaughter, influencer Alyssa Stellato, shared this inspiring video online. The post highlights his dedication and commitment to maintaining his physical well-being. This routine has garnered significant positive attention and admiration from viewers.

title: Alia Bhatt's mom, Swara Bhasker & more rally behind Wangchuk to end hunger strike
content: Film personalities are urging activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike. Concerns are growing over his deteriorating health after weeks of fasting. Shabana Azmi and Soni Razdan have appealed for his well-being and continued leadership. Other celebrities and writers also joined calls for him to end the fast. Wangchuk is protesting alleged NEET examination irregularities and demanding minister's resignation.

title: England vs Argentina FIFA World Cup 2026: TV channel, live streaming
content: England and Argentina meet in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup 2026 semifinal on July 15 in Atlanta, with Lionel Messi chasing the Golden Boot and Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham looking to keep England's title dream alive. The article covers kickoff times, TV channels, live streaming details across major countries, key tactical storylines, Messi's influence, England's reliance on Kane and Bellingham, and the rich head-to-head history between two of international football's greatest rivals.

title: Here's why Hyderabad's monsoon tanker demand is higher than summer's
content: During the monsoon season in Hyderabad, there is a notable spike in the need for water tankers due to below-average rainfall and the effects of El Nino. Apartment complexes have increased their dependency on tanker water by three times, revealing challenges posed by fast urbanization and outdated pipeline systems. Authorities are advocating for regular water audits and the adoption of rainwater harvesting practices to secure future water supplies.

title: Oil, LNG, fertilisers and maybe diamonds: What is at risk from Iran’s wider blockade threat
content: Iran has warned of wider trade disruptions beyond the Strait of Hormuz. This threat could impact crucial energy and other export flows from the region. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil and gas shipments. Asia, which receives most of these shipments, faces significant exposure. Tensions are escalating as the US conducts strikes against Iranian capabilities.

title: From Delhi to Karnataka: EC releases revised SIR schedule - check new dates
content: The Election Commission has updated the schedule for electoral roll revision in six states. Draft publication dates vary, with final rolls due in October for most. Telangana's revision will see draft publication on August 10 and finalization on October 12. Haryana and Andhra Pradesh will publish draft rolls on July 31. This exercise aims to update voter lists by adding new and removing ineligible electors.

title: 2020 Delhi riots case: Court allows Umar Khalid 2 family video meetings every week
content: A Delhi court has restored activist Umar Khalid's weekly video meetings with his family. Jail authorities had previously reduced these meetings from two to one weekly session. The court found no justification for the reduction in the number of meetings. Khalid remains in jail in connection with the 2020 northeast Delhi riots conspiracy case. This order follows the rejection of his recent bail pleas by a Delhi court.

title: Setback for Prashant Kishor! 4 Jan Suraaj leaders switch to BJP before Bankipur bypoll
content: Several Jan Suraaj leaders joined the Bharatiya Janata Party on Wednesday. This development occurs before the Bankipur Assembly bypoll scheduled for July 30. Prashant Kishor is contesting this election against the BJP candidate Neeraj Kumar Sinha. New entrants expressed their commitment to Prime Minister Modi's leadership and national development. The bypoll was necessitated after Nitin Nabin resigned from the assembly seat.

title: Why Taslima Nasreen's Kolkata return is a powder keg for Bengal politics
content: Taslima Nasreen's upcoming return to Kolkata after 20 years sparks intense political discussions. The Trinamool Congress and Bharatiya Janata Party both voiced their strong opinions regarding her visit. Exiled since 2007, Nasreen is set to participate in an anti-fundamentalism literary event this August. Her previous works and the circumstances of her exile have sent shockwaves through conversations about free speech and the intersection with religious beliefs in West Bengal.

title: 2 Varanasi highway projects worth Rs 25,44 cr approved - how they will affect commuters
content: The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs has greenlighted an ambitious infrastructure project in Varanasi, featuring a remarkable Rs 10,998.32 crore investment. The initiative is set to create a 43.2-km elevated corridor, referred to as the Varuna Expressway. This project aims to significantly alleviate traffic congestion while improving connectivity by linking NH-31 to the Varanasi Ring Road, ultimately reducing travel time and stimulating regional economic growth.

title: A ticket to the past: When buses of Bombay were truly the BEST
content: The article recalls a time when bus travel in Bombay was a pleasant experience. Legendary routes like the No. 123 offered scenic journeys along Marine Drive. Double-decker buses provided joy with open windows and a refreshing breeze. These buses served as vital lifelines for night shift workers across the city. Modern transit has replaced this cherished era of public transportation.

title: Can you legally record the police in India? Here's what law says
content: Citizens can generally record police officers performing duties in public spaces. Recording is legal if it does not obstruct police work or break other laws. Police cannot arbitrarily seize phones or force deletion of lawfully recorded videos. Uploading videos may lead to consequences if they are defamatory or invade privacy. Courts emphasize transparency while balancing individual rights and effective policing.

title: Everyone wants to kill Google Chrome, and OpenAI's Atlas shows why nobody can
content: Try to remember choosing your browser. You can't, and neither can anyone else. OpenAI killed ChatGPT Atlas nine months after launch, and it is only the newest name on a list that includes Arc, Comet, Opera and Edge. Everyone attacks the browser. Nobody attacks the default. Chrome does not stop working, and that, in the end, is the whole wall.

title: Inside India's esports boom: Why leagues, careers and homegrown IP matter more than ever
content: India has around 600 million gamers and one of the world's fastest-growing gaming markets. But industry leaders believe the next challenge is no longer attracting players - it is building leagues, careers, intellectual property and businesses that can sustain long-term growth. As investment, regulation and e-cricket gather momentum, Indian esports finds itself at a defining moment in its evolution.

title: 'Not offered with genuine devotion': UP assembly speaker on Ram temple stolen donations
content: Uttar Pradesh assembly speaker Satish Mahana linked donation theft to lack of genuine devotion. He stated that those who feel their money was stolen may not have offered it sincerely. An investigation revealed nine major procedural and security lapses enabling embezzlement of temple donations. Eight individuals associated with the donation counting process have been arrested so far.

title: How Spain ended 'Dictator' Mbappe's reign and blew France apart
content: Until they met Spain, Mbappe and Co were tearing defences apart with disdain, but from Spain they finally learnt the meaning of pain. Today’s OFFSIDE explains how Spain took France’s imperial swagger and tore it Bona-part.

title: Semicon 2.0, Varanasi projects, rail upgrades & more: 7 big decisions cleared by Cabinet
content: The Union Cabinet approved seven significant decisions on Wednesday. These approvals covered infrastructure development and semiconductor manufacturing initiatives. Railway network upgradation and mobile phone manufacturing schemes were also sanctioned. A National Investment Policy for Urea was approved to boost self-reliance. These decisions aim to foster growth across key sectors of the economy.

title: Drone warfare 2.0: Unmanned vehicles outfitted with long-range missiles deliver deep strikes
content: Drones are now equipped with missiles, extending their strike capabilities significantly. Turkey's Baykar Kizilelma and Russia's Orion drone demonstrate this new offensive power. India's Ghatak stealth drone program aims for domestic deep-strike missions without pilot risk. The United States deployed unmanned surface vessels offensively against Iranian naval facilities. Ukraine showcased complex autonomous operations using sea, air, and land-based unmanned systems.

title: Toyota Innova Hycross facelift spied testing: Expected changes, specs & more
content: There are no details of the interior yet. However, Toyota is expected to revise the interior with updated upholstery and trim materials to give it a fresher appearance. Expected features include a bigger free-standing infotainment screen, a fully digital cluster, dual-zone climate control, ventilated seats, a wireless charger, a panoramic sunroof, electric seats adjustable for front passengers and much more.

title: Who is Rafael Jodar? Spanish teen joins Team Europe for Laver Cup 2026
content: Spanish youngster Rafael Jodar is quickly becoming one of the brightest rising stars in men’s tennis. The 19-year-old has enjoyed a breakthrough season in 2026, climbing the ATP rankings and earning a place alongside some of the biggest names in the sport. His rapid rise has now been rewarded with a call-up to Team Europe for the 2026 Laver Cup in London.

title: Doctors report rise in Influenza A, Covid-19 and H1N1 this monsoon, urge caution
content: Respiratory infections like Influenza A and COVID-19 are increasing during the monsoon. Doctors advise awareness and proper medical treatment for these common infections. Vulnerable groups should seek prompt medical evaluation if symptoms worsen. Unnecessary antibiotic use contributes to antimicrobial resistance, experts warn. Simple precautions and timely testing help prevent severe complications.

title: Why do eggs in supermarkets come only in white and brown shells?
content: Eggs that are available in the supermarket shelves are mostly while and brown. While the debate around the fact that which one is better continues, the question is why are they of this colour only and why commonly colourful eggs are not consumed.

title: How Mumbai's expanding Metro network is changing homebuyer preferences
content: Metro connectivity is increasingly influencing property values in India's largest cities. Proximity to metro stations now significantly impacts apartment prices more than traditional addresses. Mumbai's property market shows a clear shift towards connectivity-adjusted value for buyers. Gurgaon's transformation into a real estate hotspot exemplifies this transit-driven appreciation. Understanding future metro routes is becoming crucial for property investment decisions.

title: 'I would've done the same': Tuchel on Argentina's dark blue jersey choice for WC semis
content: England coach Thomas Tuchel understands Argentina's choice of dark blue jersey for the World Cup semifinal. He acknowledges the role of superstition and lucky charms in elite sports.  Argentina's dark blue kit holds historical significance for past victories against England.  Coach Lionel Scaloni dismissed superstition, suggesting tradition influenced the decision.

title: Bengaluru Boy, 12, Attempts Suicide After Alleged School Assault
content: Boy's father told police that for several days before the incident, Samarth had repeatedly told his family that he was scared to go to school.

title: Ukraine Says Will Do Everything Possible To Protect Its Seaports
content: The country's main farmers' union UAC said the country had lost about a third of its capacity to export grain via its vital Black Sea ports due to intensifying Russian missile and drone attacks.

title: Vijay, Mohanlal To Share Stage For Anti-Drug Campaign: Kerala Minister
content: The announcement came after Chennithala discussed closer cooperation with Vijay to tackle drug trafficking across the Kerala-Tamil Nadu border.

title: Will Reach Delhi Without Permission: Omar Abdullah On Statehood Protest
content: The National Conference has stepped up its campaign for statehood restoration, accusing the Centre of failing to honour repeated assurances on the issue.

title: Gautam Adani Denies Any Deal Linked To US Move To Drop Charges
content: The counsel also indicated that the proposed investment could potentially form part of a resolution if US authorities chose to consider it.

title: UP Speaker Questions 'Donations Without Devotion'. Opposition Responds
content: Uttar Pradesh assembly Speaker Satish Mahana tore into the Opposition for maligning every person and the entire organisation that struggled for the Ram temple to be built

title: Centre Plans To Bring OTT Under Censor Purview: Sources Amid 'Satluj' Row
content: The government is deliberating on making censor board certification mandatory to release films and similar content on the OTT platforms, said sources.

title: TVK Worker Expelled From Party After Viral "Bribe" Video
content: The development assumes significance as it came on the very day the state government stepped up its anti-corruption campaign by directing all government offices, district collectorates, local bodies and public sector undertakings.

title: Why Iranian, Houthi Threats To Red Sea Shipping Matter More For Oil Now
content: Closure of the Bab el-Mandeb strait - gateway to the Red Sea - would open a new front in the energy crisis and Iran's overarching conflict with the US.

title: "Like My Lover": Chinese Users Bid Farewell To AI Companions
content: Chinese users of AI-powered companion bots have bid heart-rending farewells to their virtual buddies as national regulations took effect Wednesday aimed at curbing the risk of emotional dependency.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Growing Drone Wars In Myanmar
content: The junta did not invent Myanmar's drone battlefield. It learned it from its enemies. Now those enemies must learn how to defeat the very system they helped prove.

title: US Army Says Completed 90-Minute Wave Of Strikes On Iran
content: The US military said it had completed its latest wave of strikes against Iranian targets on Wednesday that "further degraded Iran's ability to attack commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz."

title: Nair Community Body Doubles Down On Criticism Against Kerala Chief Minister
content: Kerala Chief Minister VD Satheesan's attempt to put to rest speculation about his relationship with Nair Service Society (NSS) General Secretary G Sukumaran Nair has triggered a sharp response from the community leader.

title: "Are You Muslim?" Indian Man Stabbed 15 Times In US Mall
content: 48-year-old Peter Michael Larsen told investigators he deliberately targeted the victim because he was Muslim and that he "intends to kill Muslims".

title: EU Announces "Drone Deal" With Ukraine To Boost Battlefield Technology
content: Ukraine has already signed similar deals with some individual EU countries and Gulf nations looking to tap into the expertise Kyiv has developed during more than four years of war with Russia.

title: Assam Town's Move To Name, Shame Public Urinators Sparks Debate
content: The campaign uses CCTV cameras installed at identified hotspots to capture instances of public urination and littering.

title: Woman Strangled To Death By Husband In UP, Police Case Filed
content: The accused have been identified as Vijendra and his elder brother Vinod, residents of Avrai Kala Pahi village in Ballia's Bhimpura area.

title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei Funeral Highlights: Iran Begins Ali Khamenei's Funeral Procession through Tehran
